description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player featuring a large, flared horn speaker made of a wood-textured material, reminiscent of early gramophones. The horn is mounted on a robust, rectangular wooden base with a visible wood grain pattern, giving it a classic and ornate appearance. At the center of the base sits a circular turntable platter, which holds a black vinyl record with a red and gold label. The tonearm is visible, extending from the right side of the base and positioned over the record, suggesting functionality. The overall design is decorative and nostalgic, with no visible modern electronic controls or displays. The object appears solid and well-crafted, suitable as a decorative or functional piece in an urban vintage shop, park installation, or themed café.
description: A vintage wooden record player with a large flared horn, ornate wood base, and a visible vinyl record on the platter. The tonearm is extended over the record, evoking a classic gramophone style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared horn and the square wooden base with the record and platter visible at the center.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the horn, the extended tonearm, and the circular platter with the record on top.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the horn and the record platter, with the tonearm less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, the tonearm's attachment, and the record on the platter.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and centrally located on the wooden base. It appears to be made of a dark material, likely metal or plastic, and is topped with a black vinyl record featuring a red and gold label. The platter is proportionally small compared to the base and has a smooth, flat surface with no visible grooves or patterns beyond the record itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending from the right side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is straight, medium in length, and appears to be made of the same wood-textured material as the rest of the device. The tonearm is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready for playback.
